title: Zscaler, AutoZone, And Regencell Are Among Top 10 Large-Cap Losers Last Week (May 25-May 29): Are The Others In Your Portfolio?
author: Nabaparna Bhattacharya
description: Ten large-cap stocks experienced significant declines during the week of May 25-29, 2026. Zscaler led losses with a 20.06% drop following disappointing Q3 results and weak Q4 guidance. Other major losers included Boston Scientific (16.95%), AutoZone (14.21%), Regencell Bioscience (12.61%), and Venture Global (10.87%). Declines were attributed to earnings misses, analyst downgrades, and energy sector weakness following geopolitical developments.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Largest decline at 20.06% due to disappointing Q3 financial results and Q4 guidance below analyst estimates
Declined 16.83% last week with no specific catalyst mentioned
Fell 16.95% following multiple analyst price target reductions
Decreased 14.21% after Q3 earnings report with multiple analyst downgrades
Fell 13.09% last week with no specific catalyst mentioned
Decreased 9.14% due to multiple analyst price forecast reductions
Decreased 12.61% last week with no specific catalyst mentioned
Slumped 0.87% last week
Declined 11.53% as oil and gas stocks traded lower following energy price pullback and geopolitical developments
Decreased 10.87% as energy sector weakness followed Trump's Iran negotiations announcement

title: Imperial Oil Projects Up To 460,000 Barrels Per Day Of Production In 2026
author: Lekha Gupta
description: Imperial Oil released its 2026 corporate guidance, targeting 441,000-460,000 gross oil-equivalent barrels per day through strategic investments in upstream and downstream operations, with planned capital expenditures of 2.0-2.2 billion Canadian dollars.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company projects production growth, plans strategic investments, aims to increase cash flow, and has achieved highest quarterly output in over 30 years

title: 4 Canadian Oil Stocks That Are Filling the Heavy Crude Gap
author: Chris Markoch
description: The article highlights four Canadian oil companies positioned to benefit from low heavy crude inventories and increasing diesel fuel demand, focusing on companies with strong heavy crude reserves and strategic market positioning.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Large land base, dividend growth for 24 consecutive years, analysts predict 83% potential price gain
Largest transporter of Western Canadian select, stable long-term contracts, analysts forecast 33% growth
Already up 49% in 2025, strong downstream integration, Exxon Mobil majority ownership, analysts predict additional 25% upside
Extensive oil sands operations, strong diesel market positioning, up 23% in 2025, analysts forecast 47% potential price gain

title: Indspire Celebrates Indigenous Excellence With Announcement of 2025 Indspire Awards Recipients
author: Globe Newswire
description: Indspire, a national Indigenous charity, announced the 2025 Indspire Awards honoring outstanding achievements of First Nations, Inuit, and Métis leaders across various fields. The awards ceremony will take place on April 10, 2025, in Vancouver, BC.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Suncor Energy Inc. is also mentioned as a Major Sponsor, indicating their involvement in the event.
Teck Resources Limited is the Dinner Sponsor, demonstrating their participation in the event.
BHP is the Entertainment Sponsor, suggesting their involvement in providing entertainment for the event.
Cenovus Energy is a Participating Sponsor, demonstrating their support for the Indspire Awards.
Cenovus Energy is a Participating Sponsor, demonstrating their support for the Indspire Awards.
Imperial is the Culinary Sponsor, suggesting their involvement in providing the culinary experience for the event.
Rio Tinto is also a Supporting Sponsor, showing their involvement in the event.

title: Indspire célèbre l'excellence autochtone en annonçant les noms des lauréats des prix Indspire 2025
author: Globe Newswire
description: Indspire, an Indigenous charity, is hosting the 32nd annual Indspire Awards on April 10, 2025 in Vancouver. The awards recognize exceptional achievements and contributions of First Nations, Inuit, and Métis leaders across various categories.

BHP is the sponsor for the awards show, indicating their involvement in celebrating and showcasing the achievements of the Indigenous award recipients.
